title: service-accounts — PHP attribute contracts
description: One attribute. #[AsServiceAccountListener] marks a class as a subscriber to service-account lifecycle events (created, rotated, expired, dormant, disabled, suspicious). Discovered at boot by the ServiceAccountServiceProvider so services under `auth/`, `notifications/`, `activity/`, and `observability/` can react without explicit event bindings.

attributes.name: AsServiceAccountListener

params.name: events
params.description: Which service-account events this class reacts to. Wildcard `*` = every event under `Academorix\ServiceAccount\Events\*`. Specific list e.g. `['ServiceAccountSecretRotated', 'ServiceAccountExpired']` subscribes to those only. Validated against the events map at boot; typos abort application boot with a clear error naming the unknown event.

params.name: queue
params.description: Queue name for asynchronous dispatch. Set to `null` for synchronous listeners (rare — reserve for observability tap-outs where the listener writes a metric on the hot path). Common values: `notifications` (default — mail + Slack channels), `notifications-critical` (P0 events like SuspiciousServiceAccountActivityNotification), `audit` (write-heavy compliance streams).

params.name: priority
params.description: Listener execution order. Lower values run first. Use for cases where one listener's side-effect must precede another (e.g. `InvalidateJwtKidCacheListener` before `DispatchServiceAccountDisabledNotification` — the cache flush must run first so the next incoming request sees the fresh state). Framework default listeners occupy 1–99; user-defined listeners default to 100.
